content:
🚨 Cyber criminals work in seconds. Can our institutions afford to work in silos?

The recent observations of the Madhya Pradesh High Court serve as a wake-up call for India. In an era where stolen money can pass through multiple bank accounts within minutes, delayed coordination between the Police, Banks, Telecom Service Providers, and Digital Platforms often means justice arrives too late. In my latest article, I examine the Court's significant observations, the growing menace of cyber fraud across India, the legal framework under the Information Technology Act and the new criminal laws, and why a real-time, technology-driven response is essential to protect citizens in the Digital India era. This is not merely a legal issue—it is a matter of national financial security and public trust.
